operator_view: The remediation plan is structurally sound. Four planned contracts address the three Batch 1 failure modes (doc rewrite drift, steward diff overflow, challenger non-determinism) plus ledger continuity. The design preserves fail-closed behavior, repo-clean invariant, and no-orphans constraints. Minor clarifications needed on doc drift guard location and recursive split edge case, but these are documentation refinements rather than architectural blockers. Ready for Batch 2 execution.

claims.statement: Planned contracts form a coherent fail-closed gate layer around the steward/challenger execution loop. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope

Task spec lint enforces required fields (allowed_paths, api_shape, doc_mutation_mode, max_expected_diff_lines) before run start, addressing the doc rewrite drift failure mode. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Challenger fixture gate introduces deterministic validation that known-bad fixtures trigger rework path, addressing non-deterministic miss observed in Batch 1. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Diff preflight enforces 300-line cap with auto-split orchestration, directly addressing steward diff budget overflow (340 lines > 300). REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:artifacts
Attempt ledger commit maintains atomic write semantics and repo-clean gate, preserving no-orphans invariant. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope

Module boundaries are clear: lint (pre-flight validation), gate (challenger fixture), preflight (diff sizing), commit (ledger continuity). Each addresses a distinct failure mode from Batch 1. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:artifacts
Interfaces are versionable through explicit contract fields (doc_mutation_mode, max_expected_diff_lines). REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Control flow is linear: lint → (optional split) → steward/challenger → commit. No circular dependencies. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
State is explicit: ledger mutation triggers auto-commit; failure returns CLEAN_FAIL. No hidden global state. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Failure modes are defined: missing fields → fail-closed; oversized diff → split/block; fixture miss → rework trigger. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Minimal design choice: using file-level validators rather than integrating into existing modules reduces coupling and preserves reversibility. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:constraints

Doc rewrite drift guard is described in scope but not enumerated as separate planned_contract artifact; may be subsumed in task_spec_lint.py but specification is implicit. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope

Split orchestration logic must handle edge case where diff splits produce sub-tasks that themselves exceed 300 lines (recursive split). Not explicitly addressed in scope. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Auto-commit failure path (CLEAN_FAIL) must be recoverable by manual intervention while preserving no-orphans invariant; recovery procedure not detailed. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Clarify doc rewrite drift guard integration: explicitly state whether validator lives in task_spec_lint.py or requires separate module. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Add recursive split guard: if sub-task diff > 300 lines after initial split, fail with SPLIT_FAIL rather than proceeding to steward. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Document manual recovery procedure for CLEAN_FAIL that preserves repo-clean and no-orphans invariants. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:constraints

operator_view: The Batch 1 remediation plan is governance-compliant and ready for execution. The five planned contracts (task_spec_lint, challenger_fixture_gate, diff_preflight, attempt_ledger_commit) address the three manual fallback causes from Batch 1: doc rewrite drift, steward diff overflow, and challenger non-determinism. Explicit fail-closed behavior, repo-clean hard gates, and path validation guards are designed into the mechanism-level contract. No protected paths are modified. Implementation teams should ensure CWE-22 path traversal guards in allowed_paths validation and atomic write semantics in ledger commit automation are explicitly coded.

claims.statement: Fail-closed task spec lint design correctly enforces required fields (allowed_paths, api_shape, doc_mutation_mode, max_expected_diff_lines) before run start, preventing malformed task execution. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:mechanism-level implementation contract
Doc rewrite drift guard explicitly blocks whole-file rewrites for append-only tasks, addressing the INDEX.md drift observed in Batch 1 manual fallback. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:objective

Deterministic challenger fixture validation ensures known-bad artifacts trigger rework path, creating measurable guardrail testing. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:mechanism-level implementation contract
Steward diff preflight with auto-split logic enforces 300-line cap before steward invocation, preventing the 340-line overflow from Batch 1. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:objective

Attempt ledger continuity with repo-clean hard gate preserves invariant integrity; CLEAN_FAIL return on automation failure maintains fail-closed behavior. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:mechanism-level implementation contract
Plan preserves no modifications to governance protected paths per explicit constraint. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:constraints
Ephemeral artifact directories remain gitignored by protocol; durable proof requires code/tests/docs commits only. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope

allowed_paths validation in task_spec_lint must explicitly guard against CWE-22 path traversal; implementation must normalize and validate paths before use. REF: CWE-22 [ASSUMPTION: implementation will include proper path normalization]

diff_preflight must safely compute projected diff size without loading entire file contents into memory for large files. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:mechanism-level implementation contract [ASSUMPTION: streaming diff computation will be used]

Atomic write semantics in attempt_ledger_commit.py require explicit implementation (e.g., write-to-temp + atomic rename pattern). REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:mechanism-level implementation contract [ASSUMPTION: atomic rename pattern will be used]
Include explicit CWE-22 path traversal guard in task_spec_lint.py allowed_paths validation. REF: CWE-22
Document atomic write semantics for attempt_ledger_commit.py in implementation spec. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:mechanism-level implementation contract

operator_view:
Remediation plan is approved for Batch 2 execution. Four fail-closed gates address all three Batch 1 failure modes:
- Task spec lint blocks malformed requests before run start
- Doc rewrite guard prevents INDEX.md append drift
- Challenger fixture gate ensures deterministic rework trigger
- Steward preflight prevents 300-line overflow
- Ledger continuity automation preserves repo-clean invariant

First cycle of Batch 2 should validate split threshold and fixture determinism empirically. No blocking issues identified.

claims.statement: Remediation plan addresses all three Batch 1 failure modes: doc rewrite drift, steward diff overflow, and challenger non-determinism. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope

Task spec lint provides fail-closed enforcement before run start with required fields (allowed_paths, api_shape, doc_mutation_mode, max_expected_diff_lines). REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:artifacts#task_spec_lint.py
Doc rewrite drift guard explicitly addresses the INDEX.md append drift observed in Batch 1 manual fallback #1. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:scope
Deterministic challenger validation with known-bad fixture creates verifiable rework trigger, addressing Batch 1 challenger miss. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:artifacts#challenger_fixture_gate.py
Steward diff preflight + split architecture prevents the 340-line overflow that caused Batch 1 manual fallback #2. REF: AUR-BATCH1-REMEDIATION-001:artifacts#diff_preflight.py

Known-bad fixture design requires careful construction to ensure challenger determinism; if fixture is too obvious or too subtle, trigger rate may be unreliable. [ASSUMPTION] Evidence needed: fixture design validation in pre-Batch 2 dry-run.

Auto-split logic for 300-line cap assumes stable line-count estimation; structural complexity (e.g., nested indentation) may cause estimation drift. [ASSUMPTION] Evidence needed: empirical validation of split threshold during Batch 2 cycle 1.

Atomic ledger commit semantics depend on deterministic message formatting; race conditions in concurrent cycle execution could cause metadata handoff ambiguity. [ASSUMPTION] Evidence needed: concurrent execution stress test or serialization guarantee documentation.
None required for acceptance. All specified mechanisms are implementable as described and address identified failure modes with testable acceptance criteria.
